column1: # USE OF NOAA GML DATA
# These data are made freely available to the public and the scientific
# community in the belief that their wide dissemination will lead to
# greater understanding and new scientific insights. To ensure that GML
# receives fair credit for their work please include relevant citation
# text in publications. We encourage users to contact the data providers
# who can provide detailed information about the measurements and
# scientific insight.  In cases where the data are central to a
# publication
column2: coauthorship for data providers may be appropriate.

column1: # Contact:  Xin Lan (xin.lan@noaa.gov)
# File Creation: Thu Mar  5 05:00:47 2026
# See gml.noaa.gov/ccgg/trends/ for additional details.
# Values represent the average of smoothed seasonal curves and smoothed
# de-seasonalized trends of daily mean CO2 data from the four GML Atmospheric Baseline
# observatories;
# Barrow
# CO2 expressed as a mole fraction in dry air
# NOTE: In general
column2: the data presented for the last year are subject to change

column1: # depending on recalibration of the reference gas mixtures used
# control procedures. Occasionally
column2: earlier years may also be changed for the same

column1: # reasons.  Usually these changes are minor.
year
